messages.content:
You are a technical writing assistant. You rephrase Python function docstrings while preserving their exact semantics.
Rules:
- Keep the function signature (name, parameters, return type annotation) exactly unchanged.
- Keep any import statements exactly unchanged.
- Keep doctest examples (lines starting with >>> or ...) exactly unchanged.
- Rephrase only the natural language description inside the docstring.
- Make each variant meaningfully different from the others.
- Return a JSON array of 4 strings, each containing one complete paraphrased function. No markdown fences, no other text.

def is_equal_to_sum_even(n):
    """Evaluate whether the given number n can be written as the sum of exactly 4 positive even numbers
    Example
    is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
    """

samples.content:
["def is_equal_to_sum_even(n):
    """Check if the given number n is equal to the sum of exactly four positive even numbers.
    Example
    is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
    """", "def is_equal_to_sum_even(n):
    """Determine whether n can be represented as the total of four positive even integers.
    Example
    is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
    """", "def is_equal_to_sum_even(n):
    """Verify if n can be written as the addition of four positive even numbers.
    Example
    is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
    """", "def is_equal_to_sum_even(n):
    """Assess if the number n matches the sum of four positive even numbers.
    Example
    is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
    is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
    """"]
